release-date.comment: Announced/unveiled August 2023; Lite and Plus variants joined the lineup in February 2024.

status.comment: Listed on the official ASUS Tinker Board portal and stocked by Amazon and major SBC resellers (checked 2026-04-26).

open-source-hardware.comment: ASUS publishes a user manual and block diagram but no PCB design files under an OSHW license.

country-of-origin.comment: ASUS is headquartered in Taipei, Taiwan; Tinker Board 3N is an ASUS IoT product.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: RK3568 is rated up to 2.0 GHz; ASUS marketing materials cite 2.0 GHz for the Tinker Board 3N. Some Hackster coverage cited 1.8 GHz which is the lower SKU bin — using the vendor's stated 2.0 GHz.

process-node.comment: Rockchip publishes RK3568 on 22nm process; ASUS does not republish process node, so this is sourced from a Rockchip-summary site that matches the standard datasheet figure.

opengl-es-version.comment: Mali-G52 supports OpenGL ES 1.1/2.0/3.2 in vendor blob; Mesa Panfrost is conformant up to ES 3.1.

vulkan-support.comment: Mali-G52 hardware supports Vulkan 1.1 via Arm's vendor driver; open-source PanVK is non-conformant on Mali-G52 as of 2026-04, so practical Vulkan support depends on the vendor blob.

hw-video-decode.comment: RK3568 video decoder supports H.264/H.265/VP9 up to 4K@60. AV1 hardware decode is NOT supported on RK3568.

hw-video-encode.comment: RK3568 encoder supports H.264 and H.265 up to 1080p@60.

ram-max-gb.comment: Standard 3N is sold in 2 GB or 4 GB SKUs only (per ASUS user manual spec table); the 8 GB option exists on the 3N Lite variant, not the standard 3N.

emmc.comment: Optional onboard eMMC available in 32 GB or 64 GB; an eMMC-less SKU also exists.

microsd-slot.comment: MicroSD card slot present on the underside (push/pull) per the manual.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: M.2 B-key (3042/3052) slot accepts an NVMe SSD or 4G/5G LTE module via PCIe 3.0 ×1 + USB 3.0 + USB 2.0 + SIM.

sata.comment: No native SATA port on the board.

dual-ethernet.comment: 2× gigabit Ethernet on standard 3N (and 3N Plus); Lite has only one.

wifi.comment: No onboard Wi-Fi; an M.2 E-key 2230 slot is shipped with a Wi-Fi 5/6 + BT 5.0 module pre-occupying the slot per the user manual.

bluetooth.comment: Bluetooth 5.0 (2T2R) provided by the bundled M.2 E-key Wi-Fi/BT module.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: Front panel exposes a single USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A port.

usb-a-2-ports.comment: One USB 2.0 Type-A port on the front panel; an internal 10-pin header on the board exposes two more USB 2.0 lines for chassis use.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: 1× USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-C OTG (data only; not used for power input).

poe-support.comment: PoE PD support for one of the LAN ports requires installing an optional PoE module wired to the on-board PoE VCC-out / DC-in headers; not built directly into the RJ45 jack.

max-display-resolution.comment: HDMI port is rated 4096×2160@60. eDP supports 2560×1600@60 and LVDS up to 1920×1080@60.

displayport.comment: Embedded DisplayPort (eDP) connector on a 40-pin header — intended for internal panels, not a standard external DP socket.

mipi-dsi.comment: Display outputs are HDMI, LVDS, and eDP only — no MIPI-DSI connector on the standard 3N. Some marketing summaries claim MIPI-DSI but the official user manual spec table and block diagram do not show one.

mipi-csi.comment: No MIPI-CSI camera connector listed in the user manual; cameras would need to attach via USB.

audio-jack.comment: 3.5 mm headphone jack with microphone input on the front panel; internal speaker header also present.

gpio-pins.comment: 14-pin (2×7) header. Of the 14 pins, 9 are GPIO pins shared with SPI/UART/I²C alternate functions. Not Raspberry Pi compatible.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 14-pin GPIO header is not pin-compatible with the Raspberry Pi 40-pin header.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Different form factor (100×100 mm), different header pinout, different mounting holes.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: M.2 B-key 3042/3052 slot exposes PCIe 3.0 ×1 (plus USB 3.0 / USB 2.0 / nano-SIM); M.2 E-key 2230 slot exposes PCIe 2.0 ×1 + USB 2.0 and is occupied by the bundled Wi-Fi/BT module by default.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI, and UART are all available on the 14-pin GPIO header (alternate functions).

power-input.comment: Also reachable through the on-board DC-in header (also used by the optional PoE module).

idle-power-w.comment: Vendor-published 2.218 W idle from the user manual's Power Consumption table (test rig included 2× USB 3.0 + 2× USB-C peripherals + LCD/backlight + LTE module, total 37.5 W of accessory load).

load-power-w.comment: Vendor 'Burn-in' figure of 7.394 W (CPU/GPU/RAM/eMMC/microSD/LAN stress). Manual also lists a Max Load of 44.894 W which includes ~37.5 W of accessory load (USB peripherals, LCD, backlight, LTE module) — that is total system load, not bare-board load, so the burn-in figure is the better representation.

cooling-required.comment: Bundled with a low-profile pushpin heatsink; the manual also exposes a 4-pin CPU fan header for active cooling but does not mandate it for stock loads.

rtc-battery-header.comment: 2-pin BATT_CON header (Molex 51021-0200) for an external lithium RTC battery.

length-mm.comment: PCB is 100×100 mm per the dimensions section of the user manual.

form-factor-class.comment: ASUS markets it as 'NUC-sized' (4×4 inches / 100×100 mm); doesn't match Pi-standard, Pi-Zero, Pico-ITX, or Nano-ITX dimensions.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: 100×100 mm board with corner mounting holes — not the Raspberry Pi pattern.

weight-g.comment: ASUS does not publish a bare-board weight in the user manual or product page; no credible third-party measurement found as of 2026-04-26.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: RK3568 SoC has good mainline coverage (DTSI in arch/arm64/boot/dts/rockchip), and Mesa Panfrost handles the Mali-G52 GPU. ASUS ships its own 5.10.y vendor kernel for Debian 11; there is no Tinker Board 3N–specific DTS in mainline as of 2026-04, NPU has no upstream driver, and several peripherals (video codec, NPU, some PHY/PoE plumbing) require vendor patches. Treating as 'partial'.

official-os-image.comment: ASUS publishes Debian 11 (Linux 5.10.y), Yocto, and Android 12 images on the Tinker Board download portal.

distros-with-images.comment: Vendor-supplied Debian 11 (TinkerOS-style) and Yocto. Armbian's official build matrix lists older Tinker Board / Tinker Board 2 / Edge R but does not include a Tinker Board 3N image as of 2026-04-26.

bootloader.comment: Standard Rockchip TPL+SPL+U-Boot boot flow (with an SPI flash mask jumper for boot device selection).

long-term-support.comment: ASUS markets the Tinker Board 3N for industrial/AIoT use but does not publish a hard end-of-support date or guaranteed kernel maintenance window.

android-support.comment: Official Android 12 image with FOTA support.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Standard 3N base SKU (4 GB / 32 GB) priced at $179.10 at launch (CNX, Feb 2024). 2 GB SKU pricing is not consistently published by ASUS; checked 2026-04-26.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Top SKU within the standard 3N line is the 4 GB / 64 GB configuration, reported around $199. The Plus and Lite are separate models, so they are not used here. Prices verified 2026-04-26.

available-regions.comment: Sold worldwide via ASUS distributors (WayPonDEV/Amazon US, top-electronics EU, Rutronik24, youyeetoo APAC) as of 2026-04-26.

typical-lead-time.comment: Snapshot 2026-04-26: in stock at Amazon (via WayPonDEV) and major SBC distributors; ASUS list still shows all three variants.

documentation-quality.comment: ASUS publishes a polished user manual (block diagram, header pinouts, dimensions, power consumption table) plus product page; full schematics and the SoC TRM are not openly published, costing one star.

community-size.comment: Tinker Board family forums and Armbian sub-forum exist, but RK3568 community on Tinker-branded boards is much smaller than Raspberry Pi or even Radxa/Rockchip's broader ecosystem.

active-forum-discord.comment: ASUS hosts the official tinker-board.asus.com forum which still sees regular threads.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Limited official accessory range: bundled heatsink, optional PoE module, optional 4G/5G LTE M.2 module. No HAT/case ecosystem comparable to Raspberry Pi or even ROCK boards because the form factor and 14-pin header are unique.
